zh.lifeSummary: 當Gershom Clark於1753年4月29日出生在美國康乃狄克州劍橋大學默里 倫敦勒班農時，他的父親Benoni Root Clark22歲，母親Ruth Carpenter11750歲。他於1776年5月2日與Levinah Wright結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1813年2月3日在美國佛蒙特州溫莎女爵維亞黑斯費爾德享年59歲，埋葬在美國佛蒙特州溫莎女爵維亞黑斯費爾德阿斯庫特尼。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gershom Clark于1753年4月29日出生在美国康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 倫敦勒班農时，他的父亲Benoni Root Clark22岁，母亲Ruth Carpenter11750岁。他于1776年5月2日与Levinah Wright结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他去世于1813年2月3日在美国佛蒙特州溫莎女爵維亞黑斯費爾德享年59岁，埋葬在美国佛蒙特州溫莎女爵維亞黑斯費爾德阿斯庫特尼。

nameMeanings.name: Clark
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English clerk, clark ‘clerk, cleric, writer’ (Old French clerc; see Clerc ). The original sense was ‘man in a religious order, cleric, clergyman’. As all writing and secretarial work in medieval Christian Europe was normally done by members of the clergy, the term clerk came to mean ‘scholar, secretary, recorder, or penman’ as well as ‘cleric’. As a surname, it was particularly common for one who had taken only minor holy orders. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established.
Irish (Westmeath, Mayo): in Ireland the English surname was frequently adopted, partly by translation for Ó Cléirigh; see Cleary .
Americanized form of Dutch   De Klerk   or Flemish   De Clerck   or of variants of these names, and possibly also of French   Clerc  . Compare   Clerk   2 and   De Clark  .

variants.name: Clarke
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Clark  .

History: This name was brought independently to New England by many bearers from the 17th century onward. Nicholas Clarke was one of the founders of Hartford, CT (coming from Cambridge, MA with Thomas Hooker) in 1635.

variants.name: Clerk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Jacques.
Americanized form of French   Clerc   and perhaps also of the Dutch and Flemish cognates   De Klerk   and   De Clerck   or of variants of these names. Compare   Clark   3.

variants.name: Clerkin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Liam, Brendan, Dympna, Seamus.

Irish (Monaghan): from an Irish pet form of English clerk ‘cleric’ (Irish cléireach), see Clark . Alternatively, an Anglicized form of Ó Cléireacháin, ‘descendant of little cleric’, an Irish diminutive of the word. See also McClurkin , an Antrim surname from Mac Cléirchín ‘son of little cleric’.

variants.name: Calarco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Angelo, Carmelo, Fausto, Nunziato, Salvatore.
Italian (southern): probably a nickname derived from Greek kalē archē ‘good beginning’. Compare Clark 4.

variants.name: Clerc
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Odette, Sebastien, Serge.

French: occupational or status name for a member of a minor religious order or for a scholar, Old French clerc, from Late Latin clericus, from Greek klērikos, a derivative of klēros ‘inheritance, legacy’, with reference to the priestly tribe of Levites (see Levy ) ‘whose inheritance was the Lord’. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established. In the Middle Ages it was virtually only members of religious orders who learned to read and write, so that the term clerc later came to denote any literate man, a scholar (hence its modern meaning ‘scribe, secretary’ or ‘clerk’). Compare Leclerc .

variants.name: Clarkson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): patronymic from   Clark  .

nameMeanings.name: Gershom
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by a son of Moses (Exodus 2:22). It possibly means ‘exile’ (i.e. a person in exile) in Hebrew, but it is usually interpreted as ‘sojourner’, from Hebrew ger sham ‘a stranger there’.

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: British Forces Capture Fort Griswold
historicEvents.description: The capture of Fort Griswold was the final act of treason that Benedict Arnold committed. This would be a British victory. On the American side 85 were killed, 35 wounded and paroled, 28 taken prisoner, 13 escaped, and 1 twelve year old was captured and released.
historicEvents.urlTitle: History of the Battle of Groton and The Burning of New London

historicEvents.title: Shays' Rebellion
historicEvents.description: Caused by war veteran Daniel Shays, Shays' Rebellion was to protest economic and civil rights injustices that he and other farmers were seeing after the Revolutionary War. Because of the Rebellion it opened the eyes of the governing officials that the Articles of Confederation needed a reform. The Rebellion served as a guardrail when helping reform the United States Constitution.

historicEvents.title: Connecticut Becomes the 5th State
historicEvents.description: Connecticut became a state on January 9, 1788. In 1650, before it was a state, the boundary of Connecticut ran north from the westside of Greenwich Bay and the coast of the Pacific Ocean. During the 1600s, Westmoreland County was in Connecticut when the boundaries were changed Westmoreland County went to Pennsylvania.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.

historicEvents.title: Brass is Discovered
historicEvents.description: "In 1802, brass was identified in Waterbury, Connecticut. This gave the city the nickname ""The Brass City."" Brass dominated the city and helped to create the city. The motto of the city is Quid Aere Perennius, which means What is more lasting than brass? in Latin."
historicEvents.urlTitle: Connecticut History.Org- Birth of the Brass Valley

en.lifeSummary: When Gershom Clark was born on 29 April 1753, in Lebanon, New London, Connecticut, United States, his father, Benoni Root Clark, was 22 and his mother, Ruth Carpenter, was 11750. He married Levinah Wright on 2 May 1776. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 3 February 1813, in Weathersfield, Windsor, Vermont, United States, at the age of 59, and was buried in Ascutney, Weathersfield, Windsor, Vermont, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gershom Clark est né le 29 avril 1753, à Lebanon, New London, Connecticut, États-Unis, son père, Benoni Root Clark, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Ruth Carpenter, avait 11750 ans. Il a épousé Levinah Wright le 2 mai 1776. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé le 3 février 1813, à Weathersfield, Windsor, Vermont, États-Unis, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterré à Ascutney, Weathersfield, Windsor, Vermont, États-Unis.

Er hat am 2. Mai 1776 Levinah Wright geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Er starb am 3. Februar 1813 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Weathersfield, Windsor, Vermont, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Ascutney, Weathersfield, Windsor, Vermont, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Gershom Clarkが1753年4月29日にアメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ニュー ロンドンレバノンで生まれた時，父Benoni Root Clarkは22歳，母Ruth Carpenterは11750歳であった。1776年5月2日にLevinah Wrightと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1813年2月3日にアメリカ合衆国バーモント州ウィンドソルウエアスエルスフィールドで59歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国バーモント州ウィンドソルウエアスエルスフィールドアスクウトニーにて埋葬された。

ko.Lebanon, New London, Connecticut, United: 미국 코네티컷 주 뉴 런던 레바넌
ko.lifeSummary: Gershom Clark(이)가 1753년 4월 29일 미국 코네티컷 주 뉴 런던 레바넌에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Benoni Root Clark(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Ruth Carpenter(은)는 11750세였다. 그는 1776년 5월 2일 Levinah Wright(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1813년 2월 3일 미국 버몬트 주 윈드소르 웨더스필드에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 버몬트 주 윈드소르 웨더스필드 아스c트니에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gershom Clark nasceu em 29 de abril de 1753, em Lebanon, New London, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Benoni Root Clark, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Ruth Carpenter, tinha 11750 anos. Ele casou-se com Levinah Wright em 2 de maio de 1776. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele faleceu em 3 de fevereiro de 1813, em Weathersfield, Windsor, Vermonte, Estados Unidos da América, com 59 anos, e foi sepultado em Ascutney, Weathersfield, Windsor, Vermonte, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.Lebanon, Windham, Connecticut Colony, Br: лебанон, виндхам, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.Ascutney, Weathersfield, Windsor, Vermon: аскутни, витирсфиелд, виндзор, Вермонт, Соединенные штаты
ru.Lebanon, New London, Connecticut, United: лебанон, ню лондон, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gershom Clark родился 29 апреля 1753 г. в местности лебанон, ню лондон, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Benoni Root Clark было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Ruth Carpenter, 11750. Он женился на женщине по имени Levinah Wright 2 мая 1776 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 7 дочерей. Он умер 3 февраля 1813 г. в местности витирсфиелд, виндзор, Вермонт, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 59 лет и был похоронен в местности аскутни, витирсфиелд, виндзор, Вермонт, Соединенные штаты.

es.Lebanon, Windham, Connecticut Colony, Br: Lebanon, Windham, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America
es.Ascutney, Weathersfield, Windsor, Vermon: Ascutney, Weathersfield, Windsor, Vermont, Estados Unidos
es.Lebanon, New London, Connecticut, United: Lebanon, New London, Connecticut, Estados Unidos
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Gershom Clark nació el 29 de abril de 1753, en Lebanon, New London, Connecticut, Estados Unidos, su papá, Benoni Root Clark, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Ruth Carpenter, tenía 11750 años. Se casó con Levinah Wright el 2 de mayo de 1776. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 3 de febrero de 1813, en Weathersfield, Windsor, Vermont, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 59 años, y fue sepultado en Ascutney, Weathersfield, Windsor, Vermont, Estados Unidos.
